properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 22 at 5:52PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 552 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm near
Falcon, or 9 miles northeast of Colorado Springs, moving north at 5
mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and penny size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Colorado Springs, Falcon, Cimarron Hills, and Peterson Space Force
Base.
properties.areaDesc: Northern El Paso County/Monument Ridge/Rampart Range Below 7500 Ft; Colorado Springs Vicinity/Southern El Paso County/Rampart Range Below 7400 Ft

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 5:39PM MDT until July 22 at 8:45PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...Northeastern Lincoln County in east central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 845 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 537 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 1.5 inches of rain
have fallen.

- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Hell Creek, Hackberry Creek, and Dugout Creek.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Mainly rural areas of Northeastern Lincoln County.

properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 22 at 5:36PM MDT by NWS Goodland KS
properties.description:
At 536 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 10
miles southeast of Thurman, or 13 miles north of Flagler, moving east
at 20 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and penny size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to outdoor objects is
possible.

This storm will remain over mainly rural areas of northwestern Kit
Carson County.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 5:26PM MDT until July 22 at 8:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Northwestern San Miguel County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 830 PM MDT.

* At 526 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.3 and 0.6 inches of
rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 2 to 4 inches in 1
hour. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Slick Rock and Egnar and areas along Highway 141.

properties.headline: Flood Warning issued July 22 at 5:02PM MDT until July 22 at 8:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Urban area and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of west central Colorado, including the
following county, Mesa.

* WHEN...Until 800 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying
and flood-prone locations is imminent or occurring.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 502 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the warned area. Between 0.5 and 1 inch of rain
has fallen.
- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Indian Wash, Leach Creek and Colorado River.
Additional rainfall amounts of 0.3 to 0.8 inches are possible
in the warned area.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Grand Junction, Fruitvale, Clifton and Orchard Mesa.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 4:40PM MDT until July 22 at 6:00PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
At 440 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges indicated
ongoing thunderstorms producing moderate to heavy rain across the
warned area. Between 2 and 3.5 inches of rain have fallen. Flash
flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar and automated gauges.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, gulches, roads,
and poor drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Pine Junction, Pine Grove and Shaffers Crossing.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 4:09PM MDT until July 22 at 6:15PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...South Central Larimer County in north central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 615 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 408 PM MDT, Thunderstorms are ongoing in the area, with
moderate to heavy rainfall at times. Minor flooding is ongoing or

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Estes Park.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 4:00PM MDT until July 22 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The Lee and PL Gulch burn scar in...
Central Rio Blanco County in northwestern Colorado...

* Until 700 PM MDT.

* At 400 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain over the Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scar. Between 0.3 and 1
inch of rain has fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to
begin shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flow
moving through the Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scars. The debris flow can
consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding in and around the Lee and PL
Gulch Burn Scars.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around
the Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scars.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of Central Rio Blanco County
Lee and PL Gulch Burn Scars

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 11:04AM MDT until July 22 at 9:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall over the Gold Mountain Fire is
possible.

* WHERE...Gold Mountain Fire in Ouray County Colorado, including
Dexter and Cutler Creeks and Highway 550.

* WHEN...Until 900 PM MDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Excessive rainfall is possible over the Gold Mountain
Burn Scar which could lead to flash flooding and/or debris flows,
particularly along highway 550 between Cutler Creek and Dexter
Creek. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 1057 AM MDT, The probability of heavy rainfall will
increase after 12 PM this afternoon, lasting through the
evening. If thunderstorms develop over the Gold Mountain burn
scar, debris flows are likely within Cutler and Dexter Creek
drainages, including Highway 550.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
